question: When will the front-month WTI crude oil price return to our defined normalization threshold (WTI ≤ $68.26/bbl)?
event_definition: WTI crude oil price falls to ≤ $68.26/bbl
key_assumptions: WTI follows a jump-diffusion: drift μ=0.0020, vol σ=0.0588, jump prob p=0.075/day
Jump sizes ~ Normal(μ_J=-0.025, σ_J=0.240); ≤1 jump per day
Threshold τ = $68.26/bbl (2025 75th pct); event fires when WTI falls to ≤ τ
Calibrated on 19yr history (2007-2026) excluding COVID negative price anomaly
Stationarity: jump-diffusion parameters assumed constant over forecast horizon
key_uncertainties: Threshold accuracy: $68.26 is 2025 75th pct but may not reflect 2026 'normal' level
Jump intensity and scale: limited data to separate jumps from heavy-tailed diffusion
Regime shifts: current 2026 upswing may be transient or new regime
Geopolitical shocks not in historical data (e.g., major supply disruptions)
